Queen Alexandra all her life has
piled the knitting needles. All the

DR.. Mary Gordon, inspector of
prisons in London, is one of the
youngest and prettiest of suffragets
and her pictures are eagerly sought
by British editors. Now, in Eng
land, any person is free to prohibit
the printing of a personal photo
graph, and DR.. Gordon refuses to
permit the printing of her picture
until she has read the suffragist
article that is intended to 60 with
it. If the article impresses her as
fair and favorable to equal suffrage
she consents to the use of her pho
tograph.
